About

About me

I'm a content strategist and writer who works at the intersection of organisational change, executive communication, and brand narrative. My work shows up in C-suite video scripts, large-scale employee adoption campaigns, global social media frameworks, and consumer-facing product copy — sometimes in the same year.

What connects it all is a single skill: taking something complex and making it land. Whether that's an AI transformation story for a leadership audience, a platform launch designed to drive employee behaviour, or a blog post that helps a small business owner feel capable — the job is always the same. Understand what the audience needs to feel, then build the content that gets them there.

I started in long-form SEO content for Tailor Brands, moved into brand storytelling and social strategy, and now do some of my most significant work inside organisations — partnering with change management teams, executive stakeholders, and cross-functional groups who need a communicator they can trust with high-visibility, sensitive work.

Expertise

Four disciplines, one through-line

01

Content Writing & SEO Blogging

Long-form, search-optimised content that educates, ranks, and converts. Written for Tailor Brands, balancing editorial quality with strategic intent across high-volume output.

SEO Long-form B2B/SMB Editorial
02

Brand Storytelling

Building the narrative architecture behind a brand: positioning, voice, messaging frameworks. I translate a company's identity into language that resonates with its audience and holds up across every channel.

Voice & Tone Messaging Positioning Copywriting
03

Internal Comms & Change Management

Writing that moves organisations through transition. Partnering with change management teams to craft communications that are honest, empathetic, and designed to reduce uncertainty among employees.

Change Comms Stakeholder Comms Employee Engagement
04

Global Social Media Strategy

Content analysis and strategic guidance for regional teams operating under a global brand. Developing frameworks that enable local teams to create on-brand content while speaking authentically to their audiences.

Content Strategy Global–Local Analysis Governance
Selected Work

Projects & case studies

01 Internal Comms · Live Event

Lumina: A Narrative-Driven Employee Adoption Campaign

Owned the end-to-end storytelling positioning and messaging for Lumina, the launch campaign for SeekOut Grow. Framed not as an announcement but as a narrative-driven adoption campaign, the work spanned event-led storytelling, employee-facing communications, and on-the-day scripting for a large-scale live event in India. The goal was to make a new platform feel personally relevant, not just compulsory.

1,718 Employees attended
909 Profiles completed at launch
52.9% Platform adoption rate
→
02 Executive Comms · Video Production

Sutherland BPO Midyear Review: Executive Video Script and Production

When Sutherland Global's C-suite needed a midyear review of how Talent Attraction had navigated an AI transformation year, they needed more than a summary. They needed a story. I was trusted to build it from scratch — interviewing and aligning stakeholders across functions, distilling complex, cross-team progress into a single coherent narrative, writing the full video script, co-developing the visual storyboard with designers, and coordinating on-site video shoots remotely across multiple locations. The content had to be accurate enough to satisfy every team who contributed, and compelling enough to land with executives who had ten other priorities.

From the script

"It's career mobility, powered by intelligence, personalized by design. Learning is continuous, and performance drives purpose."

Scope: Executive audience · AI transformation narrative · Full script and storyboard · Cross-functional stakeholder alignment · Remote production coordination

→
03 Internal Brand Creation · Strategic Comms

Sutherland Careers: Building an Internal Brand from the Ground Up

Sutherland uses SeekOut Grow as the engine behind its employee growth and performance ecosystem — but the brief was to make it ours. Rather than leading with a third-party platform name, the vision was to build a proprietary internal brand that employees would identify with Sutherland's own future: one that signals ambition, growth, and a unified career experience rather than a software tool. I led the naming, developed the full messaging framework, created the logo and visual identity direction, and built the launch communications plan. The result is Sutherland Careers — a brand designed to grow with the platform as it expands into performance, career pathing, and development.

80% Adoption rate, Healthcare vertical launch
In progress Full org rollout underway

Scope: Platform naming · Logo and identity direction · Messaging architecture · Launch comms · Ongoing campaign development

→
04 Global Social Strategy

Sutherland Global: Social Media Content Governance Across 13 Markets

Sutherland operates social media across 13 regional markets, each with its own team, audience, and context. The challenge was building a system that gave those teams enough structure to stay on-brand globally, without stripping out the local voice that made their content work. I led the full engagement: auditing regional content performance across Facebook, TikTok, and Instagram, identifying patterns and gaps, developing strategic guidance documents telling teams what to post and why, building content frameworks and templates they could actually use day-to-day, and presenting findings and recommendations to both regional managers and global leadership. The output was a governance system that worked in both directions — accountability upward, enablement downward.

13 Regional markets audited
3 Platform reports (FB, TikTok, IG)
2 Audiences: regional teams and global leadership
→
05 Internal Comms · Change Management

Sutherland Global: Change Communication Strategy

Partnered with the internal change management team to develop a phased communication plan for a significant organisational transformation. Crafted all-staff communications, manager briefing packs, and FAQ documents designed to build trust and reduce uncertainty throughout the process.

Result: [Add your outcome — e.g. "Employee sentiment scores improved by X% following rollout"]

→
06 Content Writing · SEO

Tailor Brands: SMB Blog Content Programme

Produced a high-volume stream of long-form, SEO-optimised blog articles for Tailor Brands, targeting small business owners at various stages of building their brand. Topics ranged from logo design and brand psychology to business formation guides, written to educate, rank, and convert.

Read the articles: tailorbrands.com/blog/category/branding

→
07 Product Copywriting · Packaging

Kitsch: Packaging Copy for the Satin Lavender Weighted Eye Mask

Wrote all consumer-facing copy for the KM176 product packaging, including the "Permission to Obsess, And Here's Why" hook and the full benefit-led bullet sequence. The copy had to earn attention in-store, justify a premium price point, and translate tactile product benefits into emotional language — all within tight spatial constraints across a multi-panel dieline.

Deliverable: Retail packaging copy across front panel, benefit panel, and insert card.

→
